com.tonic.jaloc.memory.iface

Interface NativeAllocator

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    MappedAllocator, SystemAllocator

    public interface NativeAllocator
    Allocates aligned native memory blocks.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Default Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      default com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocate(long bytes)
      Allocates bytes with 8-byte alignment.
      com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocate(long bytes, int alignment)
      Allocates bytes with the given alignment.
      default com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocateZeroed(long bytes, int alignment)
      Allocates bytes with the given alignment and zeroes them.
      default boolean clearRequired() 

    • Method Detail

      • allocate

        com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocate(long bytes,
                                                     int alignment)
        Allocates bytes with the given alignment.
        Parameters:
        bytes - the payload size
        alignment - a positive power of two
        Returns:
        the block
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if bytes is negative or alignment is not a power of two

      • allocate

        default com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocate(long bytes)
        Allocates bytes with 8-byte alignment.
        Parameters:
        bytes - the payload size
        Returns:
        the block
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if bytes is negative

      • allocateZeroed

        default com.tonic.jaloc.memory.internal.MemoryBlock allocateZeroed(long bytes,
                                                                   int alignment)
        Allocates bytes with the given alignment and zeroes them.
        Parameters:
        bytes - the payload size
        alignment - a positive power of two
        Returns:
        the block
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if bytes is negative or alignment is not a power of two

      • clearRequired

        default boolean clearRequired()
        Returns:
        true if fresh blocks need explicit zeroing; allocators handing out pre-zeroed memory return false
